name: marshmallow
what_it_does: Object serialization/deserialization and validation library for Python — defines schemas to validate input, serialize objects to dicts/JSON, and deserialize dicts to objects. marshmallow features: Schema class with fields (String, Integer, Float, Boolean, List, Nested, DateTime, URL, Email), load() for deserialization+validation, dump() for serialization, ValidationError with field-specific errors, @validates and @post_load decorators, Meta class for unknown field handling, many=True for lists, field options (required, allow_none, load_default, dump_default), and integration with SQLAlchemy/Flask. Alternative to pydantic with explicit schema class.
use_cases: Agent request validation — from marshmallow import Schema, fields, validate; class AgentRequestSchema(Schema): task = fields.Str(required=True, validate=validate.Length(min=1, max=500)); model = fields.Str(load_default='gpt-4'); temperature = fields.Float(validate=validate.Range(0.0, 2.0)); schema = AgentRequestSchema(); result = schema.load(request_data) — validates and deserializes agent API request; ValidationError raised with field-specific messages on invalid input

Agent response serialization — class AgentResponseSchema(Schema): action = fields.Str(); confidence = fields.Float(); sources = fields.List(fields.Url()); timestamp = fields.DateTime(); schema = AgentResponseSchema(); json_data = schema.dump(agent_result) — serializes agent response object to dict; nested fields handle complex objects; dump() does not validate (use dump + load for round-trip validation)

Agent nested schema — class ToolCallSchema(Schema): name = fields.Str(required=True); args = fields.Dict(); class AgentStepSchema(Schema): thought = fields.Str(); tool_calls = fields.List(fields.Nested(ToolCallSchema())) — nested schemas for complex agent structures; Nested() handles recursive serialization; agent action schemas model LLM output structure

Agent @post_load for object creation — class UserSchema(Schema): name = fields.Str(); email = fields.Email(); @post_load; def make_user(self, data, **kwargs): return User(**data); schema = UserSchema(); user = schema.load({'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'a@b.com'}) — @post_load converts dict to domain object; agent data pipeline creates typed objects after validation

Agent unknown field handling — class StrictSchema(Schema): class Meta: unknown = RAISE; class FlexibleSchema(Schema): class Meta: unknown = EXCLUDE — RAISE rejects extra fields (strict API); EXCLUDE ignores extra fields (LLM output with extra keys); INCLUDE passes extra fields through; agent LLM response parsing uses EXCLUDE to ignore unknown tool output fields
not_for: High-performance validation — marshmallow is slower than pydantic; for performance-critical validation use pydantic v2 with Rust core
Type hint-based schemas — marshmallow requires explicit field declarations; for type hint-derived schemas use pydantic or attrs with validators
Complex nested default handling — marshmallow default handling can be complex; pydantic has cleaner default_factory support
best_when: ORM serialization layers, REST API input/output where schemas need to differ from model classes, or Flask/Django projects with explicit schema-over-model design patterns.
avoid_when: You need maximum performance (use pydantic v2), type hint-based schemas (use pydantic), or simple FastAPI request validation (use pydantic directly).

pricing.notes: marshmallow is MIT licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. Validation speed: ~50K objects/sec for simple schemas. pydantic v2 is 5-10x faster for same schemas. marshmallow-dataclass and marshmallow_jsonschema add overhead. Schema instantiation cached — reuse schema instances.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError with .messages dict mapping field names to error lists. ValidationError.messages['temperature'] = ['Must be between 0.0 and 2.0.']. Nested schema errors: messages['tool_calls'][0]['name'] = ['Missing data']. Field-specific error messages are human-readable. Multiple errors per field collected in list. Clear, actionable error messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Schema.load() and Schema.dump() are deterministic for same input. @post_load transformations may not be idempotent if they have side effects. Schema validation is purely functional.
known_agent_gotchas: load() and dump() are not symmetric — schema.load(data) validates AND deserializes (validates required fields, runs validators, calls @post_load); schema.dump(obj) serializes only without validation; agent code expecting dump() to validate before serializing is wrong; use schema.load(schema.dump(obj)) for round-trip validation

Unknown fields behavior defaults to RAISE in 3.x — marshmallow 3.x default is Meta.unknown = RAISE; agent code receiving extra fields from LLM output raises ValidationError unless Meta: unknown = EXCLUDE added to schema; marshmallow 2.x ignored unknown fields by default — migration from 2.x requires adding EXCLUDE explicitly

required=True vs missing/load_default differ — fields.Str(required=True) raises if field absent; fields.Str(load_default='default') allows absent field with default; agent code mixing required and optional fields must understand: required=True takes priority; required=False with load_default='x' provides default when key missing from input

Nested schema must be instantiated not just class — fields.Nested(ToolCallSchema) passes class; fields.Nested(ToolCallSchema()) passes instance; marshmallow 3.x accepts both but instance is recommended; agent code with many=True nested: fields.List(fields.Nested(ToolCallSchema())) is correct; fields.Nested(ToolCallSchema, many=True) also works

ValidationError.messages structure differs for many=True — schema.load([item1, item2], many=True) raises ValidationError; error.messages is list where error.messages[i] has errors for item i; error.messages[0] might be {} (no errors), error.messages[1] = {'field': ['error']}; agent code handling bulk validation must iterate messages list not treat as flat dict

Schema instantiation is not free — creating Schema() inside request handler creates new schema per request; schema class introspection happens at instantiation; agent FastAPI with 1000 req/sec creates 1000 schemas/sec; declare schema at module level or as class attribute: SCHEMA = AgentRequestSchema(); reuse across requests for 10x performance improvement

security_score_components.security_notes: Validation library prevents injection and malformed input at schema boundaries. ValidationError prevents invalid data from reaching business logic. Use RAISE for unknown fields to reject unexpected input. Avoid logging ValidationError.messages if fields may contain sensitive data (passwords, tokens). marshmallow does not sanitize output — XSS prevention is caller's responsibility.
